validator_assessment.weakest_link: SPY gapped DOWN (-$0.57) overnight despite all sectors gapping UP — overnight sector strength is unconvincing to institutional capital and may not follow through at cash open.
validator_assessment.top_invalidation_scenario: SPY opens flat or down and fails to hold the 689.09 slow EMA support within the first 30 minutes, immediately inverting the ribbon and eliminating the 692.29 test thesis.

primary.condition: if SPY tests 692.29 and holds (closes back below, then reclaims) OR breaks above 692.29 cleanly with volume
primary.entry_trigger: level_reclaim of 692.29 with ribbon stack intact (spread >= 30c)

secondary.condition: if SPY fails to reach 692.29 and holds ribbon above 689.09 slow EMA, then grinds sideways before a late-day push

invalidation.condition: SPY opens flat or down and closes below 689.09 slow EMA on any 5m bar within the first 30 minutes of RTH
invalidation.effect: Ribbon inverts to BEARISH, 692.29 test thesis is dead, bias flips neutral-to-bearish with 681.14 as flush target — entire bullish consensus invalidated.

swarm_predictions.claim: SPY tests 692.29 (yesterday's RTH high) within the first 90 minutes of RTH (by 11:00 ET), driven by the $2.14 proximity and falling VIX momentum
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY does not touch 692.00 before 11:00 ET

swarm_predictions.claim: VIX remains below 18.50 for the entire session, continuing the overnight -2.7% compression trend with no macro catalyst to reverse it
swarm_predictions.invalidation: VIX spikes above 18.50 at any point before 13:00 ET

swarm_predictions.claim: The BULL ribbon stack (fast EMA > slow EMA, spread >= 30 cents) remains intact through at least the first 60 minutes of RTH (through 10:30 ET), as the 129-cent spread provides substantial buffer before inversion at 689.09
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY closes below 689.09 on any 5m bar before 10:30 ET

synthesis_narrative: All three measurable specialist agents align bullish — falling VIX (17.28, -2.7% overnight), clean BULL ribbon (129-cent spread), broad risk-on sector rotation (XLK +2.26%, XLF +0.80%, XLE +1.43%), and SPY just $2.14 below the battle level at 692.29. The validator's key dissent is structural: SPY itself gapped DOWN while every sector gapped up, signaling institutional hesitation to follow overnight euphoria — this makes 692.29 a binary test, not a layup. The single most important thing to watch at open is whether SPY holds the 689.09 slow EMA in the first 30 minutes: a hold validates the bull thesis and sets up the 692.29 test; a break below 689.09 inverts the ribbon and negates the entire framework. NOTE: technical_output.json was unavailable for this replay date — confidence is capped at 65 and strength downgraded to moderate to reflect the missing 0.35-weight primary agent.

macro.one_line: VIX 17.28 MID falling -2.7% (fear compression) + zero macro events + risk-on rotation = bullish backdrop; small overnight gap-down is the lone friction signal

level_thesis.one_line: Battle level 692.29 (Active resistance) is $2.14 away with BULL ribbon intact at 129-cent spread; break of 692.29 targets 5-day Carry 696.95

internals.one_line: XLK +2.26% / XLF +0.80% / XLE +1.43% — 3-of-3 sectors above +0.5%, broad-based risk-on rotation with tech leadership confirmed

validator.top_invalidation: Overnight SPY gap-down (-$0.57) contradicts all-sectors-up = institutional non-confirmation; 689.09 slow EMA failure within first 30 min inverts ribbon and kills the 692.29 test thesis
